nodes.x: O give thanks unto the Lord; call upon his name: make known his deeds among the people.
Give thanks unto the Lord, call upon his name, make known his deeds among the people.
Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever.
Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.
It is a good thing to give thanks unto the Lord, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High:
Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him: talk ye of all his wondrous works.
Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him, talk ye of all his wondrous works.
Sing unto the Lord, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.
Let him, therefore, hearken to your counsel, and I will bless him with a multiplicity of blessings; let him be faithful and true in all things from henceforth, and he shall be great in mine eyes;
Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name Jah, and rejoice before him.
Pray unto the Lord, call upon his holy name, make known his wonderful works among the people.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.
For our heart shall rejoice in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.
Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The Lord be magnified.

be opened unto them, that they may behold things new and precious, and rejoice in the holy principles of the Gospel of God, that has been brought to light in this dispensation, by the administration of angels to thy servants, even in the latter days. Let our enemies be taken in their own snare, and fall into the pit they dig for thy people. Let confusion come upon them; may they be turned…
Seek the Lord, and his strength: seek his face evermore.
Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his face continually.
When thou saidst, Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, Lord, will I seek.
And seek the face of the Lord always, that in patience ye may possess your souls, and ye shall have eternal life.
Wait on the Lord: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the Lord.

Jesus Christ at the Center of Our Lives By Elder Jose L. Alonso Of the Seventy The profound questions of the soul, those that surface in our darkest hours and highest trials, are addressed through the unwavering love of Jesus Christ. As we journey through mortality, we are at times beset by trials: the severe pain of the loss of loved ones, the arduous fight against illness, the sting of…
Remember his marvellous works that he hath done; his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
Remember his marvellous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the Lord is gracious and full of compassion.

He possessed a noble boldness and independence of character; his
manner was easy and familiar; his rebuke terrible as the lion; his
benevolence unbounded as the ocean; his intelligence universal, and
his language abounding in original eloquence peculiar to
himself--not polished--not studied--not smoothed and softened by
education and refined by art; but flowing forth in its own native
simplicity,…
The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit: he hath left off to be wise, and to do good.
And forgat his works, and his wonders that he had shewed them.
O ye seed of Abraham his servant, ye children of Jacob his chosen.
O ye seed of Israel his servant, ye children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
But thou, Israel, art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend.

Abraham received promises concerning his seed, and of the fruit of his loins—from whose loins ye are, namely, my servant Joseph—which were to continue so long as they were in the world; and as touching Abraham and his seed, out of the world they should continue; both in the world and out of the world should they continue as innumerable as the stars; or, if ye were to count the sand upon the…

and from among them should be raised up Prophets and Seers and Revelators to teach the people of the nations of the earth, as the oracles of God. These chosen people were Abraham and his seed. Of Abraham it is written that God called him from his father's house when he dwelt in Ur of the Chaldees, and commanded him to go out from his father's house because his father was given to the ways of the…

the seed of Abraham, the children of Israel—who were called an holy nation, a peculiar people—God has sought to regulate, by His laws, those things, and to teach the people so that they may raise up unto Him a holy nation, a peculiar people, a royal priesthood. He chose Abraham from among the nations and blessed him. He promised to multiply his seed like the stars in the heaven or the sands of…
He is the Lord our God: his judgments are in all the earth.
He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.

earth are two, vie., Truth and Judgment. He explicitly declares, that He will lay judgment to the line and righteousness to the plummet. His latter-day proclamation is, “ Fear God and give glory to Him, for the hoar of His judgment is come/' The salvation that He oners is temporal, spiritual, and eternal ; and the judgments which He will inflict are also similar. As a supreme lawgiver. He claims…
And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people.
He hath remembered his covenant for ever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations.
Be ye mindful always of his covenant; the word which he commanded to a thousand generations;
And he remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his mercies.
To such as keep his covenant, and to those that remember his commandments to do them.
¶ Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.
He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever: holy and reverend is his name.
Which covenant he made with Abraham, and his oath unto Isaac;
Even of the covenant which he made with Abraham, and of his oath unto Isaac;
The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,

They made many covenants in Abraham’s 
time, and slew victims, and offered sacrifices, 
and as yet oaths were not. Whence then did 
they come in? When evil increased, when all 
was confusion, upside down, when men had 
turned aside to idolatry: then it was, then, 
when men appeared no longer worthy to be 
believed, that they called God as witness, as if 
thereby giving an adequate surety for what…
And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant:
And hath confirmed the same to Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant,
For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.
And Jacob said, Swear to me this day; and he sware unto him: and he sold his birthright unto Jacob.
He sheweth his word unto Jacob, his statutes and his judgments unto Israel.

And as he hath covenanted with all the house of Jacob, even so shall the covenant wherewith he hath covenanted with the house of Jacob be fulfilled in his own due time, unto the restoring all the house of Jacob unto the knowledge of the covenant that he hath covenanted with them.
Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance:
Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance;
And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, thus hath the Father commanded me—that I should give unto this people this land for their inheritance.
And the Father hath commanded me that I should give unto you this land, for your inheritance.

Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land of Canaan; (this is the land that shall fall unto you for an inheritance, even the land of Canaan with the coasts thereof:)
When they were but a few men in number; yea, very few, and strangers in it.
When ye were but few, even a few, and strangers in it.
Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.
And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.
And those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were six thousand and two hundred.
When they went from one nation to another, from one kingdom to another people;
And when they went from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people;
Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:
He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
And the country was divided; and there were two kingdoms, the kingdom of Shule, and the kingdom of Cohor, the son of Noah.

And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all: and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all:
He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes;
He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes,
In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.
And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord, but not as the kings of Israel that were before him.
And after that he had established himself king he did ease the burden of the people, by which he did gain favor in the eyes of the people, and they did anoint him to be their king.
In those days there was no king in Israel, but every man did that which was right in his own eyes.
Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.
Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.
And he said unto his men, The Lord forbid that I should do this thing unto my master, the Lord’s anointed, to stretch forth mine hand against him, seeing he is the anointed of the Lord.
The Lord render to every man his righteousness and his faithfulness: for the Lord delivered thee into my hand to day, but I would not stretch forth mine hand against the Lord’s anointed.

And mine hand shall be upon the prophets that see vanity, and that divine lies: they shall not be in the assembly of my people, neither shall they be written in the writing of the house of Israel, neither shall they enter into the land of Israel; and ye shall know that I am the Lord God.
Moreover he called for a famine upon the land: he brake the whole staff of bread.
And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
And it came to pass that he brake bread again and blessed it, and gave to the disciples to eat.
¶ And there was no bread in all the land; for the famine was very sore, so that the land of Egypt and all the land of Canaan fainted by reason of the famine.

Spiritual Famine By Elder Howard W. Hunter Of the Council of the Twelve One of the most striking prophecies of the Old Testament, which certainly characterizes our day, is this one spoken by Amos: “Behold, the days come, saith the Lord God, that I will send a famine in the land, not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water, but of hearing the words of the Lord: “And they shall wander from sea to…
He sent a man before them, even Joseph, who was sold for a servant:

6. As soon as they came into Egypt, they were brought down to Joseph:
but here no small fear disturbed them, lest they should be accused about
the price of the corn, as if they had cheated Joseph. They then made a
long apology to Joseph's steward; and told him, that when they came home
they found the money in their sacks, and that they had now brought it
along with them. He said he did not know…
And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
¶ And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

their hearts, that they understood temporal matters far better than the Prophet Joseph. Are these the feelings of the people at the present time? They are not, but right to the reverse. I could have said then, the same that I could say now, if Joseph was living—if he could have been believed, and confidence could have been placed in him, with regard to temporal matters, wealth would have been…
And he left all that he had in Joseph’s hand; and he knew not ought he had, save the bread which he did eat. And Joseph was a goodly person, and well favoured.
Whose feet they hurt with fetters: he was laid in iron:
His legs of iron, his feet part of iron and part of clay.
And as the toes of the feet were part of iron, and part of clay, so the kingdom shall be partly strong, and partly broken.

And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes, part of potters’ clay, and part of iron, the kingdom shall be divided; but there shall be in it of the strength of the iron, forasmuch as thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay.
Until the time that his word came: the word of the Lord tried him.
And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
¶ And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
And it came to pass that he was obedient unto the word of the Lord, wherefore he did as the Lord commanded him.
Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
The king sent and loosed him; even the ruler of the people, and let him go free.

And he took the rulers over hundreds, and the captains, and the guard, and all the people of the land; and they brought down the king from the house of the Lord, and came by the way of the gate of the guard to the king’s house. And he sat on the throne of the kings.

And he took the captains of hundreds, and the nobles, and the governors of the people, and all the people of the land, and brought down the king from the house of the Lord: and they came through the high gate into the king’s house, and set the king upon the throne of the kingdom.
And the king commanded them that they should not return; and they were angry with the king, and caused that he should suffer, even unto death by fire.
And the king, and all the people that were with him, came weary, and refreshed themselves there.
He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his substance:
And he brought in the things which his father had dedicated, and the things which himself had dedicated, into the house of the Lord, silver, and gold, and vessels.
¶ And he brought into the house of God the things that his father had dedicated, and that he himself had dedicated, silver, and gold, and vessels.
Then he made him that remaineth have dominion over the nobles among the people: the Lord made me have dominion over the mighty.
And when he shall prove himself faithful in all things that shall be entrusted unto his care, yea, even a few things, he shall be made ruler over many;
To bind his princes at his pleasure; and teach his senators wisdom.
That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
To make known to the sons of men his mighty acts, and the glorious majesty of his kingdom.
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
And he changeth the times and the seasons: he removeth kings, and setteth up kings: he giveth wisdom unto the wise, and knowledge to them that know understanding:

And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify himself in his heart, and by peace shall destroy many: he shall also stand up against the Prince of princes; but he shall be broken without hand.
Israel also came into Egypt; and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
¶ And they went up out of Egypt, and came into the land of Canaan unto Jacob their father,
And they took their cattle, and their goods, which they had gotten in the land of Canaan, and came into Egypt, Jacob, and all his seed with him:
Then Jacob went on his journey, and came into the land of the people of the east.

he celebrated the harvest festival of the first-fruits 1 with old grain, for in all the land of Canaan there was not a handful of seed (in the land), for the famine was over all the beasts and cattle and birds, and also over man. 5. And on the sixteenth the Lord appeared unto him, and said unto him, "Jacob, Jacob"; and he said, "Here am I." And He said unto him: "I am the God of thy fathers, the…
When Jacob was come into Egypt, and your fathers cried unto the Lord, then the Lord sent Moses and Aaron, which brought forth your fathers out of Egypt, and made them dwell in this place.
And he increased his people greatly; and made them stronger than their enemies.
And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.

And he also placed armies on the south, in the borders of their possessions, and caused them to erect fortifications that they might secure their armies and their people from the hands of their enemies.
For he was determined, because of the greatness of the number of his people, to overpower the Nephites and to bring them into bondage.
And it came to pass that he did deliver them, and he did show forth his mighty power unto them, and great were their rejoicings.
And many more believed because of his own word;
He turned their heart to hate his people, to deal subtilly with his servants.
And his servants conspired against him, and slew him in his own house.
And it came to pass that he was exceedingly angry with his people, because he had not obtained his desire over the Nephites; he had not subjected them to the yoke of bondage.
¶ And the servants of Amon conspired against him, and slew the king in his own house.

And he became a great hinderment to the prosperity of the church of God; stealing away the hearts of the people; causing much dissension among the people; giving a chance for the enemy of God to exercise his power over them.
¶ But they rebelled, and vexed his holy Spirit: therefore he was turned to be their enemy, and he fought against them.
He sent Moses his servant; and Aaron whom he had chosen.
So Aaron and his sons did all things which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.
And Moses told Aaron all the words of the Lord who had sent him, and all the signs which he had commanded him.

¶ But Aaron and his sons offered upon the altar of the burnt offering, and on the altar of incense, and were appointed for all the work of the place most holy, and to make an atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.

servants of the Lord had not yet been laid upon him. But we read in the thirteenth chapter of Acts, that while certain prophets and teachers were waiting before the Lord, “the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them. And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.” It is written, “No man taketh this honor…
And Moses gave the money of them that were redeemed unto Aaron and to his sons, according to the word of the Lord, as the Lord commanded Moses.
They shewed his signs among them, and wonders in the land of Ham.
¶ And Hamor and Shechem his son came unto the gate of their city, and communed with the men of their city, saying,
And they found fat pasture and good, and the land was wide, and quiet, and peaceable; for they of Ham had dwelt there of old.

And he thought scorn to lay hands on Mordecai alone; for they had shewed him the people of Mordecai: wherefore Haman sought to destroy all the Jews that were throughout the whole kingdom of Ahasuerus, even the people of Mordecai.
He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.

And Haman told them of the glory of his riches, and the multitude of his children, and all the things wherein the king had promoted him, and how he had advanced him above the princes and servants of the king.
He sent darkness, and made it dark; and they rebelled not against his word.

And it came to pass that the words which came unto Nephi were fulfilled, according as they had been spoken; for behold, at the going down of the sun there was no darkness; and the people began to be astonished because there was no darkness when the night came.
With his strong hand he stayed the powers of darkness — he provided a path f r our feet, and led us forth from the gales of death!

And it came to pass that according to his word he did destroy them; and according to his word he did lead them; and according to his word he did do all things for them; and there was not any thing done save it were by his word.
And thus the works of darkness began to prevail among all the sons of men.
He turned their waters into blood, and slew their fish.

And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.

And Moses and Aaron did so, as the Lord commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.
And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him.
He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.
Their land brought forth frogs in abundance, in the chambers of their kings.
And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the villages, and out of the fields.

And the river shall bring forth frogs abundantly, which shall go up and come into thine house, and into thy bedchamber, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneadingtroughs:
And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy servants, and from thy people; they shall remain in the river only.
¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch forth thine hand with thy rod over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of Egypt.
And the magicians did so with their enchantments, and brought up frogs upon the land of Egypt.
He spake, and there came divers sorts of flies, and lice in all their coasts.
He sent divers sorts of flies among them, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.
And he spake unto them concerning their rebellions upon the waters, and the mercies of God in sparing their lives, that they were not swallowed up in the sea.
¶ Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
While he thus spake, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them: and they feared as they entered into the cloud.
But when divers were hardened, and believed not, but spake evil of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the school of one Tyrannus.
He gave them hail for rain, and flaming fire in their land.
He gave up their cattle also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.
At the brightness that was before him his thick clouds passed, hail stones and coals of fire.
When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:
And they thirsted not; he led them through the deserts; he caused the waters to flow out of the rock for them; he clave the rock also and the waters gushed out.
In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.
He smote their vines also and their fig trees; and brake the trees of their coasts.
He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycomore trees with frost.
And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;

And when he saw a fig tree in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And presently the fig tree withered away.
For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
He cried aloud, and said thus, Hew down the tree, and cut off his branches, shake off his leaves, and scatter his fruit: let the beasts get away from under it, and the fowls from his branches:
He spake, and the locusts came, and caterpillers, and that without number,
He gave also their increase unto the caterpiller, and their labour unto the locust.
Even these of them ye may eat; the locust after his kind, and the bald locust after his kind, and the beetle after his kind, and the grasshopper after his kind.
And your spoil shall be gathered like the gathering of the caterpiller: as the running to and fro of locusts shall he run upon them.
The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;
And did eat up all the herbs in their land, and devoured the fruit of their ground.
And they went out into the fields, and gathered their vineyards, and trode the grapes, and made merry, and went into the house of their god, and did eat and drink, and cursed Abimelech.
And after they had tasted of the fruit they were ashamed, because of those that were scoffing at them; and they fell away into forbidden paths and were lost.

And they took strong cities, and a fat land, and possessed houses full of all goods, wells digged, vineyards, and oliveyards, and fruit trees in abundance: so they did eat, and were filled, and became fat, and delighted themselves in thy great goodness.
And they did pray unto the Lord their God continually, insomuch that the Lord did bless them, according to his word, so that they did wax strong and prosper in the land.
And it came to pass that they began to prosper by degrees in the land, and began to raise grain more abundantly, and flocks, and herds, that they did not suffer with hunger.
He smote also all the firstborn in their land, the chief of all their strength.
So they smote him, and his sons, and all his people, until there was none left him alive: and they possessed his land.
And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:
To him that smote Egypt in their firstborn: for his mercy endureth for ever:

¶ And it came to pass, that at midnight the Lord smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle.
¶ Hath he smitten him, as he smote those that smote him? or is he slain according to the slaughter of them that are slain by him?
He brought them forth also with silver and gold: and there was not one feeble person among their tribes.
And they had taken all their gold, and silver, and their precious things, which they could carry, and also their provisions with them, into the wilderness; and they pursued their journey.
And all they that were about them strengthened their hands with vessels of silver, with gold, with goods, and with beasts, and with precious things, beside all that was willingly offered.
Beside that which chapmen and merchants brought. And all the kings of Arabia and governors of the country brought gold and silver to Solomon.
And their father gave them great gifts of silver, and of gold, and of precious things, with fenced cities in Judah: but the kingdom gave he to Jehoram; because he was the firstborn.
And they brought every man his present, vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and raiment, harness, and spices, horses, and mules, a rate year by year.
Egypt was glad when they departed: for the fear of them fell upon them.
And all the people, both small and great, and the captains of the armies, arose, and came to Egypt: for they were afraid of the Chaldees.

¶ And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and they were sore afraid: and the children of Israel cried out unto the Lord.
Thou shalt not be afraid of them: but shalt well remember what the Lord thy God did unto Pharaoh, and unto all Egypt;
And they were also led out of captivity and out of the land of Egypt, by that same God who had preserved them.
And they shall be afraid and ashamed of Ethiopia their expectation, and of Egypt their glory.
He spread a cloud for a covering; and fire to give light in the night.
With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
So it was alway: the cloud covered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
The people asked, and he brought quails, and satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.

¶ And there went forth a wind from the Lord, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day’s journey on this side, and as it were a day’s journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.
And he pressed upon them greatly; and they turned in unto him, and entered into his house; and he made them a feast, and did bake unleavened bread, and they did eat.
He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
He opened the rock, and the waters gushed out; they ran in the dry places like a river.
He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.
And they thirsted not when he led them through the deserts: he caused the waters to flow out of the rock for them: he clave the rock also, and the waters gushed out.
He clave the rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink as out of the great depths.
He turneth the wilderness into a standing water, and dry ground into watersprings.
For he remembered his holy promise, and Abraham his servant.
¶ And Abraham gave all that he had unto Isaac.
For the promise, that he should be the heir of the world, was not to Abraham, or to his seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.
(Which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures,)

For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the Lord, to do justice and judgment; that the Lord may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.
And he brought forth his people with joy, and his chosen with gladness:
And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:

And strengthened the spirits of the righteous,
     In the name of His righteousness.
Yea, and behold, Abraham saw of his coming, and was filled with gladness and did rejoice.
Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they inherited the labour of the people;
And he gave them into the hand of the heathen; and they that hated them ruled over them.
But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their works.
And the Lord did visit them and prosper them, and they became a large and wealthy people.
They took their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses, and that which was in the city, and that which was in the field,
That they might observe his statutes, and keep his laws. Praise ye the Lord.
And it shall be with him, and he shall read therein all the days of his life: that he may learn to fear the Lord his God, to keep all the words of this law and these statutes, to do them:
And to judge his people by the testimony of the just, and by the assistance of his counselors, according to the laws of the kingdom which are given by the prophets of God.
O the greatness and the justice of our God! For he executeth all his words, and they have gone forth out of his mouth, and his law must be fulfilled.
To see to all things as it shall be appointed unto him in my laws in the day that I shall give them.
